structure_description: 14-section channel + distribution deep-dive for a single country x physical-goods industry. Front matter (cover/methodology/TOC) -> dark divider -> 2-page executive summary -> channel landscape pages (mix today + 5-year evolution) -> channel margin waterfall (centerpiece) -> geography + named distributors -> distributor economics -> disruption signals -> e-commerce subsegment penetration -> methodology endnote.

sections.purpose: Report cover — title, country/industry/year, KIRA branding, report ID, confidential tag
sections.notes: No content generation needed — pure template fill from parsed topic metadata.

sections.purpose: Two-column overview of research approach: primary distributor interviews / portfolio scrape, secondary trade-press + customs + listed-company filings, KIRA margin triangulation, source tagging conventions
Table of contents — generated from this structure plus page counter
sections.notes: Generated at assembly time, not content-gen time.

sections.purpose: Dark-mode chapter break — single thesis statement on the dominant channel structure and the biggest channel shift underway

Two-page executive summary: page 1 = 4 callouts (dominant channel share, biggest channel shift, margin-compression signal, e-commerce penetration) + 2-col narrative + 1 chart (channel share donut or top-line mix); page 2 = 5 strategic implications cards focused on channel strategy (where to invest, where to retreat, where to disintermediate, where to defend, where to enter)
sections.char_budget_hint: callout num_max=12 chars, narrative_left total_chars_max=1200, implications cards body_max=360 chars each. Implication verbs: Reweight, Disintermediate, Defend, Enter, Sequence

sections.title_pattern: Channel landscape: who moves volume today
sections.purpose: Channel mix snapshot across modern trade x traditional trade x e-commerce x direct (B2B / D2C) x instant delivery. Chart: stacked-area or grouped bar showing 3-year share trend per channel. Narrative anchors the dominant channel, the structural laggard, and the channel still pulling above-GDP growth.
sections.char_budget_hint: stacked-area chart top 55% of page, narrative bottom 45% in 2 cols

sections.purpose: Centerpiece chart #1. 5-year channel-share evolution by % of total category value. Stacked area or 100% stacked bar with annotated inflection points (e.g. instant-delivery breakout, GT share decline, marketplace consolidation). Narrative reads what flipped, what compressed, what stayed sticky.
sections.char_budget_hint: Chart dominates page; sidebar narrative max 600 chars + 3 annotated inflection callouts (each <=80 chars)

sections.title_pattern: Channel margin distribution: who keeps the gross
sections.purpose: Centerpiece chart #2. Horizontal waterfall from brand ex-factory price (100%) to consumer shelf price (100%), with each link's margin take labeled with %, source tag, and a one-line take-note. 6-9 layers depending on category (importer optional, MT/GT split if both material). Sidebar lists 3 anomalies: the fattest layer, the compression point, the disintermediation risk.
sections.char_budget_hint: Waterfall top 55%, commentary bottom 45%. Each layer: margin_pct <=8 chars, take_note <=60 chars. See manifest.yaml comment block for full channel_waterfall page-type shape.

sections.purpose: Distribution depth across urban-dense (Tier 1) x Tier 2 cities x Tier 3 towns x rural. Map or stacked bar showing % outlets reached + % volume per geography tier. Narrative: where the country's distribution white-space is, which tiers are over-served, which players over-index in each tier.
sections.skip_if: topic is a fully urban category with no rural channel (e.g. luxury, B2B-enterprise-only)
sections.char_budget_hint: Map or 4-bar chart, 2-col narrative below. Each tier callout <=120 chars

sections.purpose: 6-8 named distributors with category coverage matrix — columns: distributor name, ownership/parent, category portfolio (chips), geographic reach, exclusive principals, est. revenue or volume share. Repurposes competitive_structure page schema; rows are distributors, not brands.
sections.skip_if: fewer than 4 named distributors identifiable from research
sections.char_budget_hint: Matrix rows 6-8, category chips per row 3-6 chars each, max 4 chips/row. Sidebar header narrative <=400 chars.

sections.purpose: Benchmark page — margin %, standard payment terms (net 30/60/90), MOQ ranges, exclusivity norms (territorial vs category vs none), credit-line cycles. 4-card grid + 1 supporting bar chart on margin ranges by tier (master distributor vs sub-distributor vs retailer).
sections.char_budget_hint: 4 econ-cards (each label <=30, body <=240 chars) + bar chart bottom-half

sections.purpose: Four-quadrant or 4-card breakdown of disruption vectors: (1) D2C brand penetration, (2) marketplace consolidation + take-rate, (3) social commerce (TikTok Shop / live commerce) volume, (4) instant delivery / quick-commerce category coverage. Each card carries a leading metric + 1-line directional read.
sections.char_budget_hint: 4-card grid, each card: metric <=12 chars, label <=30 chars, body <=200 chars

sections.purpose: E-commerce share by category sub-segment — bar chart ranking sub-segments by online %, with a YoY delta column. Narrative pulls out the 2 sub-segments crossing the 20% online threshold (structural shift) and the 2 sub-segments still under 5% (channel-defensible).
sections.skip_if: topic has no meaningful sub-segment differentiation (collapse into 12_disruption_signals)
sections.char_budget_hint: Horizontal bar chart 6-10 rows, 2-col narrative <=800 chars total

sections.purpose: Source mix breakdown ([primary]/[secondary]/[estimate] percentages), key data anchors (Nielsen-style channel-share series, customs aggregates, listed-company distributor disclosures), KIRA margin-triangulation methodology disclosed, contact & next research footer
sections.notes: Required — buyers value transparent methodology, especially for margin figures which lean estimate-heavy. Auto-generated from source-tag census of the report.

assembly_rules.page_numbering: Continuous, format '012 / NN' in page-header counter slot — total NN computed at assembly
assembly_rules.section_numbering: section_num shared across all pages of a section (e.g. exec summary p1 + p2 both tagged 'Section 04')
assembly_rules.divider_placement: One dark divider opens the distribution-analysis chapter (after front matter). No further dividers — the report is tight enough that additional dividers waste pages.
assembly_rules.skip_condition_handling: If skip_if condition triggers, omit the section AND recompute page counter for all subsequent sections. Minimum surviving section count: 11 (front matter 3 + divider 1 + exec summary 2 + channel landscape 1 + mix evolution 1 + margin waterfall 1 + disruption 1 + methodology endnote 1).
assembly_rules.centerpiece_sections: Sections 06 (channel mix evolution) and 07 (margin waterfall) are the report's two visual anchors. If either fails overflow or data-coverage gate, regenerate — do not skip.
